title: Elaphrium schiedeanum
taxonomy: Elaphrium schiedeanum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae, commonly known as legumes. It belongs to the subfamily Faboideae and is part of the tribe Dalbergieae. The plant is native to Mexico and Central America, where it grows in tropical regions. Elaphrium schiedeanum is also known to be a climbing vine, using its tendrils to support itself in its habitat. Further classification at lower taxonomic levels is needed to provide more specific information about the plant's identity.

intro: Elaphrium schiedeanum, also known as Elaphrium, is a plant species native to Mexico, belonging to the Rutaceae family and used as a medicinal herb. The health benefits of Elaphrium include its potential to aid in digestion, relieve respiratory issues, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a valued remedy in traditional medicine. The bioactive constituents of Elaphrium include limonoids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
